Citation: Faruk, M. J. H., Alam, F., Islam, M., et al. (2024). Transforming online voting: a novel system utilizing blockchain and biometric verification for enhanced security, privacy, and transparency. Cluster Computing.
Method Used: Uses Hyperledger Fabric blockchain with biometric verification (fingerprint/face recognition). Identity is verified before vote casting and vote records are stored immutably on blockchain.
Research Gap: Does not clearly separate off-chain authentication and on-chain voting; no Aadhaar/national-ID integration; lacks double biometric confirmation before vote commit.

Citation: Wang, B., Guo, F., Liu, Y., et al. (2024). An efficient and versatile e-voting scheme on blockchain. Cybersecurity.
Method Used: Uses smart contracts and cryptographic vote encoding for privacy, verifiability, and decentralized vote handling.
Research Gap: Focuses on cryptographic privacy but lacks biometric authentication and real-world identity verification models.

Citation: A Decentralized Voting System on the Polygon Blockchain (2024). Procedia Computer Science.
Method Used: Implements voting via Polygon smart contracts and homomorphic encryption (Paillier) for private tallying.
Research Gap: No biometric or national identity verification; assumes trusted registration phase.

Citation: Large-scale Election Based on Blockchain (2018). Procedia Computer Science.
Method Used: Employs homomorphic encryption and ring signatures to ensure anonymity and scalability in blockchain voting.
Research Gap: Strong privacy but weak voter authenticity mechanisms; no biometric binding.

Citation: Peace engineering: The contribution of blockchain systems to the e-voting process (2021). Technological Forecasting & Social Change.
Method Used: Conceptual analysis of how blockchain improves transparency, trust, and election security.
Research Gap: Theoretical work only; lacks implementation details, biometric integration, and authentication workflow.

Citation: E-voting system using cloud-based hybrid blockchain technology (2024). Journal of Network and Systems Security Research.
Method Used: Uses hybrid blockchain architecture with digital signatures and cloud-based infrastructure for scalable voting.
Research Gap: Authentication relies on digital signatures; no biometric hardware integration or off-chain identity verification.

Citation: A Blockchain-Based E-Voting System for India: Addressing Security Challenges with Aadhaar Card Authentication (IEEE Conference).
Method Used: Integrates Aadhaar-based identity verification with blockchain voting framework for Indian election context.
Research Gap: Aadhaar mostly used at registration stage; no repeated biometric verification or explicit off-chain authentication model.

Citation: ECC-EXONUM-eVOTING: A Novel Signature-Based e-Voting Scheme Using Blockchain and Zero Knowledge Property (IEEE).
Method Used: Combines blockchain with elliptic-curve cryptography and zero-knowledge proofs for secure anonymous voting.
Research Gap: No biometric identity linkage; assumes trusted enrollment and lacks real-world hardware authentication.

Citation: BlockVOTE: An Architecture of a Blockchain-based Electronic Voting System (IEEE).
Method Used: Provides blockchain architecture emphasizing immutability, auditability, and smart-contract-driven voting.
Research Gap: Focuses on blockchain layer only; does not solve voter authentication or biometric validation.

Citation: Secure E-Voting System using Blockchain Technology and Authentication via Face Recognition and Mobile OTP (IEEE).
Method Used: Uses face recognition and OTP-based authentication before vote submission to blockchain.
Research Gap: Software-based authentication only; no hardware biometrics, Aadhaar integration, or off-chain privacy-preserving verification.
